summary: Iteration 4 (final retest of copyRef fix). Verified the Reference panel copy button now uses the robust copyToClipboard helper from /app/frontend/src/lib/clipboard.js. Confirmed via grep that NO `navigator.clipboard` references remain in Editor.jsx — only the helper import. Ran the full happy-path Playwright flow exactly as specified in the review request and ALL acceptance criteria passed.

test_results.navigate_to_manuscript_ms_cbc1b8ae6fda4a: PASS (route is /manuscript/:id, not /editor/:id — used correct route)
test_results.copy_button_state_change: PASS — text changed from 'Copy as APA' -> 'Copied APA' (real browser clipboard succeeded)
test_results.toc_introduction_navigation_after_copy: PASS — click succeeded, no overlay blocking

critical_code_review_comments: copyToClipboard helper in /app/frontend/src/lib/clipboard.js is well-implemented: try/catch wraps navigator.clipboard, falls back to execCommand textarea trick, always returns boolean — never throws. This is the right pattern for sandboxed/iframe preview contexts.

Editor.jsx copyRef now correctly awaits the helper and branches UI state on the boolean (Copied APA / Copy blocked). Verified via grep that there are no remaining direct navigator.clipboard.writeText calls in Editor.jsx.
Note: The review request mentioned route '/editor/ms_cbc1b8ae6fda4a' but the actual route in App.js is '/manuscript/:id'. Not a bug — just an FYI in case future test specs need adjusting.
context_for_next_testing_agent: copyRef bug is fully resolved. The button now shows 'Copied APA' in real browser and would show 'Copy blocked' in headless environments where execCommand also fails — either is acceptable. No further retesting required for this fix.
rca of the issue: Previous iterations 1-3 found that copyRef in Editor.jsx was calling navigator.clipboard.writeText() directly, which throws an unhandled NotAllowedError inside sandboxed preview iframes, triggering the webpack-dev-server overlay. Fix: extracted a robust copyToClipboard helper that wraps the async API in try/catch and falls back to a hidden-textarea + document.execCommand('copy') path, always returning a boolean. copyRef now awaits this helper and renders the success or 'Copy blocked' state purely from the returned boolean — no exception ever escapes.
